title: UDLM Realized Entity (instance)
description: The operational record of one entity (or Composite Entity) as it flows through the four states. This is the data substrate DCM reads and writes — UDLM carries the records, DCM applies the policy that produces them (core-tenets G3). It complements the Resource Type Specification (which defines the TYPE); this defines an INSTANCE. Field values inside the state snapshots are typed by the entity's Resource Type Spec, not re-validated here.

uuid.description: Stable RFC 9562 identity of this entity. A Composite Entity has exactly one UUID across all four states (entities/composite-service-model.md).

type_version.description: Pins the Resource Type Specification version this instance was realized against (E5). Drift and validation are measured against this exact contract.

type_ref.description: The `$id` of the pinned Resource Type Spec.

lifecycle_state.description: The entity's current state in the four-state model (foundations/four-states.md).

observed_generation.description: The `generation` the Realized state reflects. observedGeneration < generation ⇒ realization pending (the K8s drift signal).

states.description: An immutable snapshot per state. Intent is captured before processing; Requested is the assembled, policy-validated, provider-ready payload; Realized is the provider-confirmed snapshot (system of record); Discovered is the actively-introspected state used for drift.

provenance.description: FIELD-LEVEL PROVENANCE (#191 / E4): keyed by dot-path into the spec, an ordered history of who set each field. UDLM carries this record; DCM writes it during assembly (a Layer, a Policy, an actor) and at realization (a Provider). It is the same record the audit chain consumes (G3) — never a parallel model.

actor.description: The authenticated identity on whose behalf the change was made.

timestamp.description: RFC 3339 instant, normalized to UTC ('Z'), seconds precision minimum (common-elements.md §Timestamps). The producing clock is attributed via the nearest time_source.

previous_value.description: Value before this change (any JSON type), for the audit diff.

ownership.description: FIELD OWNERSHIP (R4 / server-side apply): keyed by dot-path, the manager that owns each field. Enables offline conflict detection when multiple vendor-agnostic providers write one record (no central apiserver needed). Distinct from provenance: ownership = who is authoritative now; provenance = the full history.

dependencies.description: Resolved instance-level edges (the realized form of the type's relationships). A binding records the value pulled from the target's realized output — data movement, not transformation (T4).

bound_field.description: The field on this entity the bound value populated (binds_to data movement, T4). Never a relation label — relation semantics live in `relation`.

resolved_at.description: RFC 3339 instant, normalized to UTC ('Z'), seconds precision minimum (common-elements.md §Timestamps). The producing clock is attributed via the nearest time_source.

target_handle.description: ADVISORY handle of the target at reference time (identifier-scheme §3). target_uuid is authoritative; a handle mismatch on resolution is drift (REF-002), not failure.

strength.description: depends_on only. hard = target unavailability blocks this entity; soft = degrade-don't-break (entities/service-dependencies.md DEP-006) — informs ordering but never blocks.

relation.description: The declared relation name from the pinned type's relationship vocabulary (relationships[].name) — REL-001: must be declared; REL-003: refines, never overrides, kind's ordering semantics, so graph consumers that read only kind+strength stay correct. NOT bound_field (which is strictly the data-binding field of a binds_to edge).

constituents.description: For a Composite Entity: the constituents and their per-component realized state (entities/composite-service-model.md).

drift.description: The diff between Discovered and Requested. A non-empty `fields` means observed reality diverged from intent.

checked_at.description: RFC 3339 instant, normalized to UTC ('Z'), seconds precision minimum (common-elements.md §Timestamps). The producing clock is attributed via the nearest time_source.

sovereignty.description: The sovereign placement of the realized entity. These are realized from `immutable`/createOnly type fields — they cannot change without replace + Governance-Matrix re-evaluation (cross-cutting P4).

status.description: Realized-state health (OSAC-/K8s-style). Reported by the provider; not part of the four-state axis.

last_transition_time.description: RFC 3339 instant, normalized to UTC ('Z'), seconds precision minimum (common-elements.md §Timestamps). The producing clock is attributed via the nearest time_source.

audit.description: Linkage to the tamper-evident audit log (AUD-001/002). UDLM carries the reference (the Merkle head + leaf count); DCM produces and proves the chain (G3).

log_head.description: Hash of the latest audit leaf for this entity (RFC 9162 Merkle).

adopted_standards.description: ADOPTED-STANDARD BINDINGS (T5): for each external standard adopted by this entity's type, the realized record of DCM's version negotiation — the requested range, the provider's chosen version, the negotiated effective version, and whether DCM translated between versions (ADS-003/004/008). UDLM carries this record; DCM (Policy) produces it and writes it to the audit log. The external data itself is NOT carried here — it is referenced from its Information Provider via the identity join.

handle.description: ADVISORY human-readable identity (contracts/identifier-scheme.md §3). The uuid is the authoritative identity; the handle aids review and drift detection (a resolved-uuid whose current handle disagrees with a reference's recorded handle is drift, not failure). Never resolve by handle alone.

metadata.description: Human-facing context. Operational commentary lives in `notes` — it is data ABOUT the record, never used for resolution.

notes.description: Auditable operational notes: each entry is attributed and timestamped — never an anonymous mutable blob.

at.description: When recorded. RFC 3339 instant, normalized to UTC ('Z'), seconds precision minimum (common-elements.md §Timestamps). The producing clock is attributed via the nearest time_source.

author.description: Who/what recorded it (person, agent+session, or process uuid).

correlation_ids.description: Stable natural keys independent of any observer, for entity resolution across discovery sources (dcm ADR-017 Decision C): a new observation matching an existing entity by these keys MERGES into that entity's uuid — one real resource, one entity uuid, never a duplicate. Every discovery source MUST emit them.

scheme.description: The kind of natural key. provider-id = the owning provider's own resource identifier (Avenue-1 discovery).

standard.description: External standard name, e.g. FOCUS, OpenCost.

requested_version.description: The version pin/range the consumer or type required.

provider_version.description: The version the bound provider emits/consumes (from its support matrix).

effective_version.description: The version actually used after negotiation (the system-of-record fact).

translated.description: True when DCM translated between providerVersion and effectiveVersion (ADS-004) — a Policy act, lowering confidence for derived values.

translation_ref.description: Reference to the deterministic migration used (preferably the standard's own published migration).

provider_uuid.description: The provider / Information Provider that served the adopted data (lookup-only; not owned here — ADS-007).

resolved_at.description: RFC 3339 instant, normalized to UTC ('Z'), seconds precision minimum (common-elements.md §Timestamps). The producing clock is attributed via the nearest time_source.

fields.description: Spec field values at this state (typed by the Resource Type Spec).

at.description: RFC 3339 instant, normalized to UTC ('Z'), seconds precision minimum (common-elements.md §Timestamps). The producing clock is attributed via the nearest time_source.

time_source.description: Attribution of the clock that produced `at`: host + sync discipline (e.g. 'host01 chrony, stratum 2, NTP-traceable') or the auditable carrier a backfilled instant came from (e.g. 'git commit <sha> committer clock, NTP-synced'). Fabricated precision is prohibited — a backfilled timestamp must name its carrier (common-elements.md §Timestamps).

origin.description: How this snapshot came to be: declared (the forward flow — consumer/provider wrote it), discovered-derived (synthesized from observation, dcm ADR-017 Decision B — provider-agnostic inferred Intent for DR/portability), backfilled (tertiary Requested/Intent synthesis during greening). Consumers weight trust accordingly; inferred state is never silently presented as human-declared.

outputs.description: Typed realized outputs (E2) published by the provider.

at.description: RFC 3339 instant, normalized to UTC ('Z'), seconds precision minimum (common-elements.md §Timestamps). The producing clock is attributed via the nearest time_source.

provider.description: The provider that produced this realized state.

time_source.description: Attribution of the clock that produced `at`: host + sync discipline (e.g. 'host01 chrony, stratum 2, NTP-traceable') or the auditable carrier a backfilled instant came from (e.g. 'git commit <sha> committer clock, NTP-synced'). Fabricated precision is prohibited — a backfilled timestamp must name its carrier (common-elements.md §Timestamps).

origin.description: How this snapshot came to be: declared (the forward flow — consumer/provider wrote it), discovered-derived (synthesized from observation, dcm ADR-017 Decision B — provider-agnostic inferred Intent for DR/portability), backfilled (tertiary Requested/Intent synthesis during greening). Consumers weight trust accordingly; inferred state is never silently presented as human-declared.
